query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "qwen-vla: unifying vision-language-action modeling across tasks, envir" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism (Vision-Language-Action modeling) and domain (navigation tasks with VLA models) as the user's question, specifically addressing the critical issue of "path deviation" and "hallucinations" which are central to extending or improving VLA systems like Qwen-VLA. Even though it focuses on a specific diagnostic mechanism (attention heads) rather than the full unification architecture, it provides essential prior work
